OK, hold on a moment b/c I asked Claude.AI to help me understand the core concept of futon theory & I think we need to add a new flexiarg complement that to futonic-logic that explains this aspect.  Here are the rough notes on etymology:

Joe> Can we discuss the etymology of 蒲团

Claude responded: 蒲團 (simplified 蒲团, Mandarin pútuán) is one of those compounds where both halves are doing concrete, picturable work, and the literal sense is almost completely…Excavated Chinese character origins and Buddhist meditation cushion historyExcavated Chinese character origins and Buddhist meditation cushion history蒲團 (simplified 蒲团, Mandarin pútuán) is one of those compounds where both halves are doing concrete, picturable work, and the literal sense is almost completely transparent once you take it apart.
蒲 (pú) is the marsh plant — cattail / bulrush / rush, Typha. The character is a phono-semantic compound: the grass radical 艹 on top tells you it's a plant, and 浦 (pǔ, "riverbank, water's edge") underneath supplies the sound while also gesturing at the wet habitat. So even the graph encodes "the reed that grows by the water." These rushes were the standard weaving material for mats and cushions

OK, great.  So, this one I think we can run as a standard mission.  E.g. we could insert a HEAD something like this:

We've talked a lot about different ways of tracking *goals* — ranging from the original devmaps to missions, excursions, and a proof-is-in-the-pudding reality-based detection system. turns.head: *Maybe* we should be thinking about not only how to make  M-
turns.full: *Maybe* we should be thinking about not only how to make  M-autoclock-in work but how to set up a tiny DSL that can be used to mint new sorries &c on the fly, rather than needing to break stride or process every turn with an agent twice.  In my PhD thesis Appendix F I quoted Deleuze & Guattari "[I]t is by headlong flight that things progress and signs proliferate." — This is the kind of "flight" that I'm referring to with the War Machine by the way — "Fuite covers not only the act of fleeing or eluding but also flowing, leaking, and disappearing into the distance (the vanishing point in a painting is a point de fuite). It has no relation to flying"

turns.head: This is the right instinct, and I think it's a real architec
turns.full:
This is the right instinct, and I think it's a real architectural pivot — not a tweak. Let me take the Deleuze seriously, because it names exactly what's wrong with what we just spent the session building.

**The extractive pipeline is the State apparatus.** Everything we did — run a 70B over every turn, *twice*, to *recover* the sorries/memes/C-entries that were implicit — is capture-after-the-fact: it lets the work flow past, then sends an agent to *represent* what flowed. That's why it's expensive, why ~50% came back junk we had to gate, and why it "breaks stride." The structure was never actually missing — the agent *knew* it was minting a goal when it acted; it just didn't **declare** it, so we pay a GPU to guess it back. Reterritorialization.

**The DSL is the line of flight.** *Fuite* — flow, leak, the vanishing-point — is structure **leaking out of the work as the work happens**, at the speed of doing, rather than being stopped and measured. A tiny inline notation an agent (or you) emits mid-flow that **mints** the object at the point of creation, parsed **deterministically, CPU, no LLM** — that's the whole point: *not* "process every turn with an agent twice." Signs prolif

Folded in as §0, the conceptual keystone — and it resolves the two threads of this whole arc into one idea:

The **extractive pipeline captures**; a **futon woven in the flow concentrates**. That's the difference between the two senses doing one job each (anticipation *or* representation) and doing **both at once**: a futon minted on the line of flight is simultaneously an *atom-of-the-future* (it grounds work to come) and a *蒲團* (a woven-concentrated form that, re-encountered, reproduces the state that made it). **Scatter → weave-and-concentrate → reproduce.** And the precise unification of this session's two threads is the one line: **recognizing the symbolic act (咅/香) *is* the weaving** — drawing scattered live acts into a concentrated invariant as they happen.

The sharp consequence I drew out — and recorded as a VERIFY criterion — is what separates this from everything we've been gating: **a minted futon must be a *support*, not a log entry.** Re-encountering it has to *re-induce orientation* (sense iii — the cushion produces concentration in its user), or it's mere capture wearing the name. turns.head: Recorded — and the seed-coding did more than apply a notatio

Recorded — and the seed-coding did more than apply a notation; it **diagnosed what the recognition vocabulary needs**. Two findings:

**The 2014 apparatus already anticipated the WM's attention-organizations** — and the matches are striking, not forced:
- **☀️ *personal attention* = AIF precision / R7** (Appendix F literally has a glyph for *attention*),
- **🔢 *problem-solving maps to interaction-history & curation* = the mining pass** (a glyph for turn-history mining),
- 🔀 *association* = the ambient pattern-layer (香), 📊 *progressive problem-solving* = the devmap.

That's an 11-year resonance, and it's not luck: your 2014 apparatus was about *para-mathematical community design* — attention organized around collaborative knowledge-building — which is the **same shape** as the WM. The reuse works *because* they're the same kind of thing. (Which retroactively validates the whole "attention-organization lineage" framing.)

**Where it runs out is the signal.** Appendix F codes attentional *themes* (nouns) but has **no glyph for the line of flight** (generative proliferation — the WM building itself) and **none for the act / composition** (咅 articulation, 鹽 composition — *verbs*). That

turns.full: Right, so, let's review: every turn should have a sigil attached; in fact, sigils are two-part: a paired Tokimoji and TokiZH hanzi symobl.  It's the same underlying vocabulary — Tokipona — a sigil is just a two-word phrase, with the register distinct.  In fact we could read them as "upper trigram" vs "lower trigram" except instead of a trigram we have loads of bits, so it's much more expressive.  Anyway, all turns have this, but what we don't have is the "flow" into a control layer — exactly the meta-language that we've been thinking about in our mining passes and in M-points-de-fuite
